script:
the work is the way


the work is the way


Every morning in Rome, before the city woke, Marcus Aurelius sat at a small writing table and opened his journal. Not to read. To write. He did this for years — the same table, the same hour, the same hand moving across the page. No one was watching. No one was meant to. He was not building a legacy. He was doing the next thing that was his to do.


the work is the way


This morning, your thing is this:

finish the OATH spec without doomscrolling first


Open the laptop.

Close every other tab.

One document. One hour. Nothing before it.


the work is the way


Begin.

Every morning before Rome woke, Marcus Aurelius sat alone in the dark — not in a throne room, not at a banquet. At a writing desk. Candle lit. He wrote to no one. He wrote to himself. Obligations he owed himself. Pages of them. Before the court. Before the petitions. Before any of it.


the work is the way


Finish the OATH spec without doomscrolling first.

Open the laptop.

Close every other tab.
